subject: science
question: Name the process of converting vegetable oil to vegetable ghee.
answer: Hydrogenation

subject: science
question: Give reason for the statement – Since the ovary releases one egg every month, the uterus also prepares itself every month by making its lining thick and spongy.
answer: Required for nourishing the embryo if fertilization takes place and the fertilized egg reaches the uterus.

subject: science
question: Name the component of white light that has the greatest wavelength.
answer: Red
chapter: The Human Eye and the Colourful World

subject: science
question: Which natural resource are the ‘biodiversity hot spots’? Suggest what happens when there is a loss of biodiversity?
answer: Forests are biodiversity hot spots. Loss of biodiversity leads to loss of ecological stability.

subject: science
question: Write the name and molecular formula of an organic compound having its name suffixed with ‘–ol’ and having two carbon atoms in the molecule. With the help of a balanced equation indicate what happens when it is heated with excess of conc. H₂SO₄.
answer: Ethanol, C₂H₅OH (or C₂H₆O). CH₃CH₂OH → (conc. H₂SO₄, heat) CH₂=CH₂ + H₂O. (Ethanol dehydrates to ethene.)

subject: science
question: A cube of edge 6 cm is placed over a printed page. At what distance from the top surface of cube will the letters appear when seen from above? Refractive index of glass is 1.5.
answer: Apparent depth = Real depth / Refractive index = 6 / 1.5 = 4 cm.

subject: science
question: What is meant by isomers? “We cannot have isomers of the first three members of alkane series” — give reason to justify this statement. Draw the structures of two isomers of pentane, C₅H₁₂.
answer: Isomers are compounds which have same molecular formula and different structural formula. In first three members of alkane, branching is not possible, therefore isomers are not possible. Two isomers of pentane: n-pentane (CH₃-CH₂-CH₂-CH₂-CH₃) and 2-methyl butane (CH₃-CH(CH₃)-CH₂-CH₃).

subject: science
question: i) Name the following: a) Third member of aldehyde series b) Second member of carboxylic acid series. ii) Write the IUPAC name of the following: a) CH₃-C(CH₃)₂-CH₃ (2,2-dimethylpropane structure) b) CH₃-CH(OH)-CH₂-CH₃ (2-butanol structure).
answer: i) a) Third member of aldehyde series: Propanal (CH₃CHO). b) Second member of carboxylic acid series: Ethanoic acid (CH₃COOH). ii) a) 2,2-dimethyl propane. b) 2-Butanol.

subject: science
question: Four elements A, B, C and D have atomic numbers 12, 13, 14 and 15 respectively. Answer the following questions giving reasons: i) What is the number of valence electrons and valency of D? ii) Which of them will have largest atomic radii? iii) Which of these elements will form the most basic oxide?
answer: i) D has atomic number 15; electronic config. 2,8,5; valence electrons = 5; valency = 3. ii) A (atomic number 12) will have largest atomic radii as it has fewest protons in this group and is leftmost in the period. iii) A (atomic number 12, Mg) will form the most basic oxide as it is the most metallic.

subject: science
question: Differentiate between combustion and oxidation reactions of ethanol.
answer: Combustion: Complete burning of ethanol in the presence of excess of oxygen to give carbon dioxide and water. CH₃CH₂OH + 3O₂ → 2CO₂ + 3H₂O. Oxidation: Addition of oxygen to ethanol in the presence of oxidizing agents to give ethanoic acid. CH₃CH₂OH → (Alk. KMnO₄, heat) CH₃COOH.

subject: science
question: In a germinating seed, which parts are known as future shoot and future root? Mention the function of cotyledon.
answer: Future shoot: Plumule. Future root: Radicle. Cotyledon: Stores food for the future plant or embryo.

subject: science
question: What is placenta? Write any two major functions of placenta.
answer: Placenta is a disc-shaped organ or special tissue in the uterus of pregnant mammal, nourishing and maintaining the foetus through the umbilical cord. Functions (any two): a) Provides large surface area for glucose and oxygen to pass from mother to the embryo. b) Removal of waste generated in the developing embryo into the mother’s blood.

subject: science
question: ‘Different species use different strategies to determine sex of a newborn individual. It can be environmental cues or genetically determined’. Explain the statement by giving example for each strategy.
answer: Environmental Cue: In some animals, the temperature at which fertilized eggs are kept determines whether the developing animal in egg is male or female; in some animals like snail, individual can change sex. Genetical Cue: A child who inherits an X chromosome from her father will be a girl and one who inherits a Y chromosome from the father will be a boy.

subject: science
question: A 2 cm high object is placed at a distance of 32 cm from a concave mirror. The image is real, inverted and 3 cm in size. Find the focal length of the mirror and the position where the image is formed.
answer: m = v/u = he/ho = -3/2 = -1.5; so v = -1.5 × (-32) = 48 cm. Using mirror formula: 1/f = 1/v + 1/u = 1/(-48) + 1/(-32) = -5/96; f = -19.2 cm. Image is formed 48 cm in front of the mirror.

subject: science
question: A child while playing with his father’s spectacles burnt a hole in a piece of paper by focusing a small image of the sun on it. (i) What defect of vision his father is suffering from? (ii) Write two causes for this defect. (iii) Draw a ray diagram to show image formation by the defective eye. (iv) Draw a ray diagram showing corrected eye using proper lens.
answer: (i) Hypermetropia. (ii) Two causes: (a) Focal length of eye lens is too large. (b) Eyeball becomes too short. (iii) Ray diagram showing image forming behind the retina. (iv) Ray diagram showing correction using a convex (converging) lens.
chapter: The Human Eye and the Colourful World

subject: science
question: Explain the formation of rainbow in the sky with the help of a diagram. List the three phenomena involved in proper sequence.
answer: Water droplets act like small prisms. They refract and disperse the incident sunlight, then reflect it internally and finally refract it again when it comes out of a rain drop. Due to dispersion and internal reflection, different colours reach the observer’s eye along different paths. Three phenomena in proper sequence: refraction, dispersion, internal reflection of light.
chapter: The Human Eye and the Colourful World

subject: science
question: Which types of people are benefited from the development of different resources? Why do we need to use the resources carefully?
answer: Rich and powerful people are benefited. We need to use resources carefully because: they are not unlimited; the demand is increasing due to human population growth; for sustainable development.

subject: science
question: Write any three characteristics of a food chain. Dharmesh always treated the chemical effluent before disposing it in the water body. Mention any three moral values possessed by Dharmesh.
answer: Characteristics of food chain (any three): (i) Unidirectional. (ii) Helps in understanding the food relationship and interaction among various organisms in an ecosystem. (iii) Helps to understand movement of toxic substances and the problem of their biological magnification. Moral values of Dharmesh (any three): Sensitive towards environment; knowledge about biological magnification; scientific temperament; conscious/eco-friendly.

subject: science
question: In the following table, six elements A, B, C, D, E and F of the modern periodic table with atomic numbers 3 to 18 are given in the table. a) Which of these is i) Noble gas ii) Halogen. b) Which of these is the most active metal in 3rd period? c) Identify the most electronegative element in the third period. d) In the compound between B and F what type of bond will be formed? e) What would be the nature of oxide formed by C?
answer: a) Noble gas: G; Halogen: F. b) Most active metal in 3rd period: B (Na, atomic number 11). c) Most electronegative in 3rd period: F (Cl, atomic number 17). d) Ionic bond. e) Oxide formed by C (Mg, atomic number 12) would be basic.

subject: science
question: A. Draw a longitudinal section of a flower and label the following parts: a) Part that develops into a fruit b) Part that produces pollen grain c) Part that transfers male gametes d) Part that is sticky to trap pollen. B. Name the parts labeled A, B, C, D in the diagram given below (showing pollen grain, pollen tube, ovary, female gamete).
answer: A. Labelled diagram required: a) Ovary b) Anther c) Pollen tube d) Stigma. B. A – Pollen grain; B – Pollen tube; C – Ovary; D – Female gamete.

subject: science
question: A. How does speciation take place? B. Define the term GENE. C. The gene for red hair is recessive to the gene for black hair. What will be the hair colour of a child if he inherits a gene for red colour from his mother and a gene for black hair from his father? Express with the help of flow chart.
answer: A. Speciation may take place by: (i) Migration (ii) Natural selection (iii) Mutation (iv) Genetic Drift (any two). B. Gene: Segment of DNA which is functional and is made of nucleic acids and protein. C. Red hair – Mother – Recessive (bb); Black hair – Father – Dominant (BB). Cross: BB × bb → F1 = Bb (Black hair). The child will have black hair.

subject: science
question: A. Mention any two points of difference between acquired and inherited traits. B. If the tail of a mouse is cut for twenty one generations, will the tail occur in the twenty second generation of that mouse? Give reason to support your answer. C. Define the term – Natural Selection.
answer: A. Acquired Traits: Not present in genetic make-up; not inheritable. Inherited Traits: Present in genetic make-up; inheritable (any two differences). B. Yes, the mouse will continue to have a tail because it is an acquired trait — cutting the tail does not change the DNA, so information for presence of tail persists in the DNA. C. Natural Selection: Nature selects the best trait in a species, leading to survival of the fittest and evolution of species.

subject: science
question: (i) Rohit claims to have obtained an image twice the size of object with a concave lens. Is he correct? Give reason for your answer. (ii) Where should an object be placed in case of a convex lens to form an image of same size as of the object? Show with the help of ray diagram the position and the nature of the image formed. (iii) With the help of ray diagram, illustrate the change in position, nature and size of the image formed if the convex lens in case of (ii) is replaced by concave lens of same focal length.
answer: (i) No, magnified image of an object cannot be formed by a concave lens ever. (ii) Object should be placed at 2f. Image is real, inverted and of same size. (iii) With concave lens, image is virtual, erect and diminished.

subject: science
question: (i) Which property of concave mirror is utilized for using them as shaving mirrors? (ii) Light passes through a rectangular glass slab and through a triangular glass prism. Using proper ray diagram, explain in what way does the direction of the two emergent beams differs with respect to the incident beam of light. (iii) A concave lens has a focal length of 50 cm. Calculate its power.
answer: (i) When an object is placed between the pole and focus of concave mirror, a magnified, erect and virtual image is obtained. (ii) In a rectangular glass slab, emergent rays are always parallel to the direction of incident rays. In a prism, incident light bends towards the base, so incident ray and emergent ray are not parallel. (iii) f = −50 cm = −0.5 m; P = 1/f = −2 D.

subject: science
question: A student is given equal amount of three samples A, B and C of water with temporary hardness. He keeps the three samples at different temperatures. A at room temperature, B at 50°C and C at 80°C. Which sample will give maximum amount of lather with soap solution? a) A b) Both B and C c) Only C d) Both A and B
answer: c) Only C

subject: science
question: Soaps are formed by alkaline hydrolysis of a) Carboxylic acids b) Esters of long chain fatty acids c) Esters of small chain d) Inorganic acids
options: Esters of long chain fatty acids
answer: b) Esters of long chain fatty acids

subject: science
question: On addition of Na₂CO₃ to hard water a) the foaming capacity increases b) the foaming capacity decreases c) the foaming capacity increases then decreases d) the foaming capacity decreases then increases
options: the foaming capacity increases then decreases
the foaming capacity decreases then increases
answer: a) the foaming capacity increases

subject: science
question: In finding the focal length of a distant object by concave mirror, you are required to draw the ray diagram of nature.
answer: Parallel beam of light, after reflection, meets at focus.

subject: science
question: While performing the experiment to trace the path of a ray of light passing through a rectangular glass slab, four students labeled their ray diagrams in the manner shown below. The correct labeling has been done by student: (a) (b) (c) (d) [student options shown in diagram]
answer: (a) — correct labeling as per MS

subject: science
question: A convex lens forms a virtual image when an object is placed at a distance of 20 cm from it. The focal length (a) f = 40 cm (b) f = 20 cm (c) f > 20 cm (d) f < 20 cm
answer: c) f > 20 cm (when the object is placed between F and P, virtual image is obtained)

subject: science
question: In a dicot seed, the pore through which the seed absorbs water during seed germination is called a) Micropyle b) Hilum c) Funicle d) Radical
answer: a) Micropyle

subject: science
question: For the experiment – To prepare temporary mount of yeast to study budding process; Yeast granules are made to first grow by adding them to a) Hydrochloric acid b) Distilled water c) 10% sugar solution d) Alcohol
answer: c) 10% sugar solution

subject: science
question: Two slides were shown to four students, and they labeled them as following. Student I: Slide A – Budding in Yeast, Slide B – Binary fusion in Amoeba. Student II: Slide A – Binary fission in Amoeba, Slide B – Buds of Amoeba. Student III: Slide A – Binary fission in Amoeba, Slide B – Daughter cells of Amoeba. Student IV: Slide A – Budding in Amoeba, Slide B – Daughter cell of Yeast. The student who made the correct labeling is: a) II b) I c) IV d) III
answer: d) III

subject: science
question: With regard to turnip, carrot, sweet potato and potato, three belong to the same category. Identify those three modifications and mention whether they are homologous or analogous. Also mention the reason why the fourth modification does not belong to the same category.
answer: Turnip, Carrot, Sweet Potato are modified roots – Homologous organs. Potato is a modified stem, so it does not belong to the same category as the other three.

subject: science
question: A student has to perform an experiment on tracing the path of a ray of light passing through a rectangular glass slab for three different angles of incidence. Two of his friends suggest the following options to him: (A) Draw the incident rays corresponding to 20°, 50° and 70° as angles of incidence and fix the two pins on the incident ray just 2 cm apart. (B) Draw the incident rays corresponding to 30°, 45° and 60° as the angles of incidence and fix two pins on the incident rays nearly 8 cm apart. Which is the better option he should follow? Give reason in support of your answer.
answer: Option B is better. For better results, the angle of incidence should be in the range 30°–60° and larger separation between pins will give better collinearity of pin and accuracy of result.

subject: science
question: A student obtained a sharp image of a lighted candle on a screen using a convex lens. Now he wants to focus a distant lamp on a far away electric pole. In which direction should the lens be moved for this purpose with respect to the screen, to get a sharp image on the screen? Justify your answer.
answer: He should move the lens towards the screen. As the distance of the object increases, the image formed by a convex lens will be closer to the focus.
